name: Sour Cream Raisin Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook raisins in a small amount of water until softened.
2. Reserve 1/4 cup of the raisin juice.
3. In a separate bowl, mix together the remaining ingredients (sugar, cornstarch, egg yolks, egg whites, salt, cream of tarter, and vanilla) until well combined.
4. Cook the mixture over low heat until it thickens. Stir constantly to prevent scorching.
5. Remove from heat and stir in the reserved raisin juice and cooked raisins.
6. Pour the filling into a pre-baked pie crust.
7. Make the meringue topping by beating together the egg whites, vanilla, and sugar until stiff peaks form.
8. Spread the meringue over the top of the pie filling.
9. Place the pie in the oven and bake just long enough to lightly brown the meringue.
Note: Be careful not to overcook the custard mixture as it can easily burn. Remove from heat immediately after thickening.
